title: Objects should not be created to be dropped immediately without being used
description: Objects should not be created to be dropped immediately without being usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoCreating an object without assigning it to a variable or using it in any function means the object is essentially created for no reason and may be dropped immediately without being used. Most of the time, this is due to a missing piece of code and could lead to an unexpected behavior.If it’s intended because the constructor has side effects, that side effect should be moved into a separate method and called directly. This can help to improve the performance and readability of the code.new MyConstructor(); // Noncompliant: object may be droppedDetermine if the objects are necessary for the code to function correctly.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

noncompliant: new MyConstructor(); // Noncompliant: object may be dropped

checks.message: Objects should not be created and immediately dropped - assign to variable if needed
